_comment: ADO Service Hook configurations for Zapier integration. Two hooks needed: one for alert created, one for state changed.

serviceHooks.name: GHAzDO Alert Created → Zapier (Create Work Item)

event.description: Fires when a new GHAzDO security alert is detected (secret, code scan, or dependency)

publisherInputs._comment_repository: Leave empty for all repos, or set specific repo GUID

zapierNotes.webhookUrl: Get this from Zapier Zap #1 → Catch Hook trigger step
zapierNotes.testPayload: Use ADO Service Hook 'Test' button to send sample event to Zapier

serviceHooks.name: GHAzDO Alert State Changed → Zapier (Close Work Item)

event.description: Fires when a GHAzDO alert state changes (e.g., active → fixed, active → dismissed)

publisherInputs._comment_repository: Leave empty for all repos, or set specific repo GUID

zapierNotes.webhookUrl: Get this from Zapier Zap #2 → Catch Hook trigger step
zapierNotes.testPayload: Use ADO Service Hook 'Test' button to send sample event to Zapier

nativeZapierConsumer._comment: ADO Service Hooks list Zapier as a native consumer, but it only supports standard ADO events (work item, code push, build, release). GHAzDO advancedsecurity.alert.* events are NOT available through the native Zapier consumer.
nativeZapierConsumer.reason: GHAzDO Advanced Security events (advancedsecurity.alert.created, advancedsecurity.alert.stateChanged) are not included in the native Zapier consumer's event list. These are newer event types specific to GitHub Advanced Security for Azure DevOps.
nativeZapierConsumer.workaround: Use the Web Hooks consumer (as configured above) to POST GHAzDO events to Zapier's Catch Hook URL.

steps: 1. In Azure DevOps, go to Project Settings → Service Hooks
3. Select 'Web Hooks' as the consumer (NOT 'Zapier' — see nativeZapierConsumer note)
4. For hook #1: Event = 'Advanced Security alert created'
5. For hook #2: Event = 'Advanced Security alert state changed'
6. Paste the respective Zapier webhook URL in the URL field
8. Click 'Test' to verify Zapier receives the payload
setup.permissions: Project Administrator or Service Hook Administrator role required
